Title: Complete replacement of Arabidopsis oil-producing enzymes with heterologous diacylglycerol acyltransferases

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates diacylglycerol with acyl-CoA to form triacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: contextualized transgenic expression in Arabidopsis dgat1-1; seed fatty acid profiling; oil content measurement; polar lipid analysis; segregation-based complementation of dgat1-1/pdat1-2 lethality; Y2H; BiFC; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds; pollen-relevant expression conferred by AtDGAT1 promoter and first intron
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtDGAT1 promoter with AtDGAT1 first intron; native-like/inducible developmental expression
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtDGAT1; CsDGAT1; PfDGAT1; RcDGAT1; AtLPCAT2; AtPDAT1; AtPDCT
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restores dgat1-1 seed fatty acid composition and oil content to near wild-type and rescues dgat1-1/pdat1-2 lethality
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Only slight differences from wild type in 18:2, 18:3, and 20:1 content
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous DGAT1 for replacing endogenous Arabidopsis TAG synthesis with minimal penalty; supports engineering of seed oil composition using native AtDGAT1 regulatory unit
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsDGAT1 was active in Arabidopsis dgat1-1 seeds under AtDGAT1 regulatory elements. 2. It restored fatty acid composition, oil content, and transcriptional changes close to wild type. 3. It rescued the lethal dgat1-1/pdat1-2 combination, indicating functional replacement of endogenous TAG synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein identity to AtDGAT1 is 91.63%; native Camelina pathway contribution is mainly PC-derived DAG route.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates diacylglycerol with acyl-CoA to form triacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: contextualized transgenic expression in Arabidopsis dgat1-1; seed fatty acid profiling; oil content measurement; polar lipid analysis; segregation-based complementation of dgat1-1/pdat1-2 lethality; Y2H; BiFC; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds; pollen-relevant expression conferred by AtDGAT1 promoter and first intron
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtDGAT1 promoter with AtDGAT1 first intron; native-like/inducible developmental expression
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtDGAT1; PfDGAT1; AtLPCAT2; AtPDAT1; AtPDCT; PfTAGL1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restores dgat1-1 oil content to near or above wild-type, shifts seed oil toward increased 18:1, 18:2, 20:1 and reduced 18:3, and rescues dgat1-1/pdat1-2 lethality
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Minor transcript alterations remained, including elevated DES6, LPCAT2, and AAPT2 relative to wild type
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising heterologous DGAT1 for metabolic engineering where altered acyl selectivity is desired without loss of viability
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PfDGAT1 functionally replaced Arabidopsis DGAT1 in seeds. 2. It restored oil content and substantially corrected the dgat1-1 fatty-acid phenotype while imparting a distinct 18:1-enriched profile. 3. It rescued dgat1-1/pdat1-2 lethality and retained interactions with Arabidopsis lipid-metabolic partners.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein identity to AtDGAT1 is 82.57%; native Physaria system is associated with TAG remodeling and unusual hydroxy-fatty-acid metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates diacylglycerol with acyl-CoA to form triacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: contextualized transgenic expression in Arabidopsis dgat1-1; seed fatty acid profiling; oil content measurement; polar lipid analysis; segregation analysis for lethality rescue; Y2H; BiFC; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds; intended pollen-relevant expression via AtDGAT1 promoter and first intron, but full functional rescue not achieved
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtDGAT1 promoter with AtDGAT1 first intron; native-like/inducible developmental expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces active TAG synthesis in dgat1-1 seeds with unique oil profile, partial restoration of oil content, increased 18:1/20:1 and VLCFA, but fails to rescue dgat1-1/pdat1-2 lethality
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Incomplete oil restoration; persistent elevated PUFA/VLCFA; altered transcriptional landscape; incompatibility with Arabidopsis system causing failure to rescue pollen lethality
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful for modifying VLCFA/PUFA features, but likely requires gene stacking or additional interactors/regulatory tuning to function fully in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcDGAT1 is enzymatically active in Arabidopsis dgat1-1 seeds and changes TAG composition in a distinct way. 2. It only partially restores oil content and drives strong transcriptional reprogramming of lipid genes. 3. It cannot replace the combined essential functions of AtDGAT1 and AtPDAT1, likely due to cross-species incompatibility and altered interactome.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein identity to AtDGAT1 is 67.61%; in Y2H it interacted strongly with itself and AtPDAT1 but not AtPDCT or AtLPCAT2, whereas BiFC also detected RcDGAT1-AtLPCAT2. The paper discusses possible splice-site incompatibility and interactome mismatch.
